title: Blinding Lights
lyrics:
[Intro]
Yeah

[Verse 1]
I've been tryna call
  > [#1, 187 votes] The first line of this song represents Abel’s vulnerability. A theme explored throughout the rest of this song. On the song “Coming Down,” Abel expresses very similar feelings: Pick up your phone The...
I've been on my own for long enough
  > [#2, 168 votes] Abel’s been on his own since around the age of 17 when he dropped out of high school and left home to never come back. This is referenced on many of his songs, like “Rolling Stone,” from his mixtape...
Maybe you can show me how to love, maybe
I'm goin' through withdrawals
  > [#3, 146 votes] A “withdrawal” can both mean the behavior in which one prefers to be alone or also the physical and mental effects experienced when a person stops using a drug. Considering Abel’s historic of drug...
You don't even have to do too much
  > [#4, 8 votes] Abel may be talking about opiate withdrawal. During opiate withdrawal, your body is extremely sensitive. Because of this, even the slightest touch could turn him on.
You can turn me on with just a touch, baby
  > [#5, 49 votes] Abel expresses his great sensitivity towards the girl he’s with to show he’s really in love with her, or that he clings to every sign of affection, following what he says in the verse. Also, even if...

[Pre-Chorus]
I look around and
  > [#6, 278 votes] Las Vegas is nicknamed “Sin City” due to the city’s overwhelming presence of crime, drugs, and sexual promiscuity—all important themes throughout The Weeknd’s career. He also explores hedonism on his...
Sin City's cold and empty (Oh)
No one's around to judge me (Oh)
I can't see clearly when you're gone

[Chorus]
I said, ooh, I'm blinded by the lights
  > [#7, 269 votes] This song directly follows the outro of “Faith,” where The Weeknd speaks about being in the back of a police car presumably after an overdose: I ended up in the back of a flashing car With the city...
No, I can't sleep until I feel your touch (Touch)
I said, ooh, I'm drowning in the night
Oh, when I'm like this, you're the one I trust
Hey, hey, hey

[Verse 2]
I'm running out of time
  > [#8, 40 votes] This line has several meanings: First, he alludes to Las Vegas again. As mentioned in the pre-chorus of the song, Abel has a special emotional connection to the city. He recalls it here and...
'Cause I can see the sun light up the sky
So I hit the road in overdrive, baby, oh
  > [#9, 87 votes] Here, Abel alludes to his habit of driving over the speed limit, which he has mentioned in several songs in the past, such as on “Heartless,”“ featured on his fourth studio album, After Hours: I’ve...

[Pre-Chorus]
The city's cold and empty (Oh)
  > [#6, see above]
No one's around to judge me (Oh)
I can't see clearly when you're gone

[Chorus]
And I said, ooh, I'm blinded by the lights
  > [#7, see above]
No, I can't sleep until I feel your touch (Touch)
I said, ooh, I'm drowning in the night
Oh, when I'm like this, you're the one I trust

[Bridge]
I'm just coming back to let you know (Back to let you know)
  > [#10, 149 votes] This line is very similar to the song “Heartless,” released just days before this song. Here, Abel describes how his love for whom is presumed to be Bella Hadid is much stronger than he can say on...
I could never say it on the phone (Say it on the phone)
Will never let you go this time (Ooh)

[Chorus]
I said, ooh, I'm blinded by the lights
  > [#7, see above]
No, I can't sleep until I feel your touch (Touch)
Hey, hey, hey

[Post-Chorus]
Hey, hey, hey

[Outro]
I said, ooh, I'm blinded by the lights
  > [#7, see above]
No, I can't sleep until I feel your touch
